items.question: Define precision and recall and give their formulas.
items.answer: Precision = TP/(TP+FP): among predicted positives, the fraction truly positive. Recall = TP/(TP+FN): among actual positives, the fraction detected. Both require a fixed threshold and labeled evaluation data.

items.question: What is F1 and why is it a harmonic mean?
items.answer: F1 = 2×(precision×recall)/(precision+recall). The harmonic mean penalizes lopsided performance—if either precision or recall is very low, F1 stays low even when the arithmetic average looks acceptable.

items.question: When would you optimize for precision instead of recall?
items.answer: When false positives are expensive or annoying: wrongful fraud holds on customers, spam filters deleting important mail, or automated legal holds on non-violating documents. You accept missing some positives to keep flagged items trustworthy.

items.question: How do macro-F1 and micro-F1 differ?
items.answer: Macro-F1 averages per-class F1 equally—highlights rare-class performance. Micro-F1 aggregates TP/FP/FN globally then computes F1—dominated by frequent classes and equals accuracy for single-label multiclass. Always state which you report.
